intents.response:
Hi! I'm Aria, your TechGiants library assistant.

I can help you:
• **Find & suggest books** by genre, topic, or mood
• **Check your borrowed books** and due dates
• **Answer questions** about borrowing, returning, and your wallet

What would you like today?

Here's how to borrow a book:

1. Go to the **Bookstore** tab
2. Click on any book you like
3. Click **Request Borrow**
4. Choose how many days you want (1–30)
5. Add an optional note

The admin reviews and approves your request. Once approved, the book appears in your **Now Reading** tab — borrowing is completely free!

To return a book:

1. Go to the **Now Reading** tab
2. Click the book you want to return
3. Click **Return Book**
4. Select the condition (excellent / good / fair / bad / damaged)
5. Add a description if the condition is bad or damaged

Always return before or on the due date to avoid any penalties!

You can extend a book's due date:

1. Go to **Now Reading** tab
2. Click the book → click **Request Extension**
3. Choose extra days (max **7 days**) and give a reason

You can extend up to **2 times** per borrowing. The admin must approve it, so request early!

keywords: how much money do i have
intents.response:
Your wallet is used for optional paid features like **PDF downloads** (Rs 400 per book) and **extension fees** (Rs 80/day).

Borrowing books is free — no wallet balance needed for that!

To top up your wallet:
1. Find the **Wallet** section (bottom of sidebar)
2. Enter the amount you want to add
3. Confirm the top-up

Your current balance is shown at the bottom of the sidebar at all times.

**Borrowing books is free!** No fee is charged when you borrow.

Your wallet is only used for:
• **PDF downloads** — Rs 400 per book
• **Extension fees** — Rs 80 per extra day
• **Lost book charges** — if a book is reported lost

So you can borrow and read without spending anything!

If a book is not returned by its due date, it becomes **overdue** and will be marked in red in your **Now Reading** tab.

To avoid this:
• Return on time
• Or request an **extension** before the due date (max 7 days, up to 2 times)

Contact the admin if you're having trouble returning a book.

We have books across many genres:

• **Fantasy** — LOTR, Dune, Harry Potter, Game of Thrones
• **Fiction** — 1984, The Alchemist, Pride and Prejudice
• **Science Fiction** — The Martian, Ender's Game, Foundation
• **Self-Help** — Atomic Habits, Deep Work, Rich Dad Poor Dad
• **History** — Sapiens, Guns Germs and Steel, Homo Deus
• **Biography** — Steve Jobs, Becoming, Elon Musk
• **Design** — The Design of Everyday Things, Don't Make Me Think
• **Technology** — Clean Code, The Pragmatic Programmer
• **Business** — Zero to One, Start with Why, The Lean Startup
• **Science** — A Brief History of Time, A Short History of Nearly Everything

Ask me to suggest books from any of these!

I'd love to find you something great! Give me a little more to go on:

• **Genre** — Fantasy, Fiction, Sci-Fi, Self-Help, Biography, History, Business, Technology?
• **Mood** — *"I'm feeling bored"*, *"something uplifting"*, *"I'm stressed"*?
• **Topic** — Space, magic, leadership, history, psychology, startups?
• **Author** — Any favourite writer?

For example:
• *"best fantasy book"*
• *"books about productivity"*
• *"suggest me something, I'm feeling down"*
• *"something by Tolkien"*

If a book is currently borrowed, you can join the **waitlist**:

1. Go to the **Bookstore** tab
2. Open the book you want
3. If it's borrowed, you'll see a **Join Waitlist** button
4. Click it — you'll be notified when the book becomes available

You'll receive a notification as soon as it's returned and ready for you!

You can download the **PDF version** of any book in our library:

1. Go to the **Bookstore** tab
2. Open any book
3. Click **Download PDF**
4. Rs **400** will be deducted from your wallet

Make sure your wallet has enough balance before downloading. You can top up from the sidebar at any time.

Here's the complete fine breakdown at TechGiants Library:

**Overdue Fines**
• Rs **100 per day** for every day past the due date
• Charged automatically when the book is returned

**Damage Fines** (based on admin's condition assessment on return)
• Excellent / Good — No fine ✅
• Fair condition — Rs **200**
• Bad condition — Rs **500**
• Damaged — **80% of the book's price**

**Extension Fees**
• Rs **80 per day** (max 7 total extra days per borrow)

**Lost Book**
• **Full replacement price** of the book, charged on admin confirmation

**PDF Downloads**
• Rs **400** per book (permanent digital copy)

💡 Borrowing is always free — charges only apply to overdue returns, damage, extensions, PDFs, or lost books!

**Returning a damaged book — what you need to know:**

When returning, you select the book's condition. Here are the **damage fines**:

• **Excellent / Good** — No fine ✅
• **Fair condition** — Rs **200**
• **Bad condition** — Rs **500**
• **Damaged** — **80% of the book's price** (e.g. Rs 1,200 for a Rs 1,500 book)

**How to return:**
1. Go to **Now Reading** tab
2. Click the book → **Return Book**
3. Select the honest condition
4. Add a description *(required for bad or damaged)*
5. Submit — the admin verifies and charges the fine

⚠️ **Important:** The admin determines the actual condition on inspection — the final fine is based on the admin's assessment, not just your selection. Always be honest!

💡 Handle books with care to avoid any damage charges.

**Lost a borrowed book? Here's what to do:**

1. Go to the **Now Reading** tab
2. Click the book → **Report as Lost**
3. Confirm — the admin is notified immediately

**What happens after reporting:**
• Admin reviews and confirms the loss
• The **full replacement price** of the book is deducted from your wallet
• The borrowing is closed and marked as lost

**All fine types for reference:**
• Overdue — Rs 100/day
• Fair condition — Rs 200
• Bad condition — Rs 500
• Damaged — 80% of book price
• **Lost — full book price**

💡 **Found the book before admin confirms?** Contact the admin immediately to cancel the report and avoid the charge!

Need help? Here's what you can do:

• **For book issues** — Check the **Now Reading** tab for your current borrowings
• **For account issues** — Open your **Profile** from the sidebar
• **For billing/fine disputes** — The admin reviews all returns and charges
• **For general questions** — Just ask me! I can answer most library questions

If you have a serious issue, look for the admin contact in the library notice board.

To manage your account:

1. Click your **profile picture or name** at the bottom of the sidebar
2. Your profile panel will open
3. From there you can:
   • View your wallet balance and top up
   • Check your reading history
   • See your borrowed books and due dates

For password changes or account issues, contact the library admin.

books.title: The Lord of the Rings
books.author: J.R.R. Tolkien
books.description: An epic high fantasy following hobbit Frodo Baggins on a quest to destroy the One Ring and defeat the Dark Lord Sauron.

books.title: The Hobbit
books.author: J.R.R. Tolkien
books.description: A fantasy novel about the adventures of Bilbo Baggins in Middle-earth on a quest to reclaim a dwarf kingdom from a dragon.

books.title: Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone
books.author: J.K. Rowling
books.description: A young boy discovers he is a wizard and enters Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry where he learns of his remarkable destiny.

books.title: A Game of Thrones
books.author: George R.R. Martin
books.description: The first book in A Song of Ice and Fire, following the power struggle for the Iron Throne of the Seven Kingdoms of Westeros.

books.title: Dune
books.author: Frank Herbert
books.description: Set in a distant future, Paul Atreides navigates political intrigue, religion, and survival on the desert planet Arrakis.

books.title: The Name of the Wind
books.author: Patrick Rothfuss
books.description: The autobiography of the legendary wizard Kvothe, widely known as the most notorious figure his world has ever seen.

books.title: The Chronicles of Narnia
books.author: C.S. Lewis
books.description: Seven fantasy novels set in the world of Narnia where animals talk, magic is real, and the great lion Aslan watches over all.

books.author: George Orwell
books.description: A dystopian novel set in a totalitarian society ruled by Big Brother, following Winston Smith's rebellion against oppressive surveillance.

books.title: To Kill a Mockingbird
books.author: Harper Lee
books.description: Young Scout Finch watches her father Atticus defend a Black man falsely accused of a crime in the American South, exploring justice and morality.

books.title: The Great Gatsby
books.author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
books.description: A portrait of the Jazz Age in all its decadence, centering on the mysterious millionaire Jay Gatsby and his obsession with Daisy Buchanan.

books.title: Pride and Prejudice
books.author: Jane Austen
books.description: The romantic story of Elizabeth Bennet and Mr. Darcy, exploring love, class, and social expectations in Regency England.

books.title: The Alchemist
books.author: Paulo Coelho
books.description: A philosophical novel about a young shepherd named Santiago who travels from Spain to Egypt in search of his personal legend.

books.title: Brave New World
books.author: Aldous Huxley
books.description: A dystopian novel set in a future World State of genetically modified citizens and an intelligence-based social hierarchy.

books.title: Animal Farm
books.author: George Orwell
books.description: A satirical allegory about farm animals who rebel against their farmer, only to find corruption and tyranny emerge from within.

books.title: The Kite Runner
books.author: Khaled Hosseini
books.description: A story of friendship, betrayal, and redemption set against the backdrop of Afghanistan from the 1970s to the early 2000s.

books.title: The Da Vinci Code
books.author: Dan Brown
books.description: Symbologist Robert Langdon investigates a murder in the Louvre and uncovers a trail of clues hidden in the works of Leonardo da Vinci.

books.title: Ender's Game
books.author: Orson Scott Card
books.description: Child prodigy Ender Wiggin is trained at a space military academy to lead Earth's forces against an alien invasion.

books.title: The Martian
books.author: Andy Weir
books.description: Astronaut Mark Watney is stranded alone on Mars after a fierce storm and must use his ingenuity and humor to survive.

books.title: Foundation
books.author: Isaac Asimov
books.description: A group of scientists preserve the knowledge of humanity as the Galactic Empire crumbles around them.

books.title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
books.author: Douglas Adams
books.description: The comedic adventure of Arthur Dent, the last surviving human, swept into a wildly improbable journey across the galaxy.

books.title: Ready Player One
books.author: Ernest Cline
books.description: In a dystopian future, teenager Wade Watts discovers clues to a massive fortune hidden inside a virtual reality game.

books.title: Atomic Habits
books.author: James Clear
books.description: An easy and proven way to build good habits and break bad ones, using tiny changes that deliver remarkable results.

books.title: The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People
books.author: Stephen Covey
books.description: A framework for personal and professional effectiveness built on seven core habits that align character and competence.

books.title: Think and Grow Rich
books.author: Napoleon Hill
books.description: One of the best-selling self-help books of all time, distilling the philosophy of personal achievement from 500 successful people.

books.title: How to Win Friends and Influence People
books.author: Dale Carnegie
books.description: Timeless advice on building relationships, communicating effectively, and becoming a more likable and influential person.

books.title: Deep Work
books.author: Cal Newport
books.description: Rules for focused success in a distracted world. The ability to perform deep work is becoming rare and increasingly valuable.

books.title: Rich Dad Poor Dad
books.author: Robert Kiyosaki
books.description: A personal finance classic contrasting two fathers' financial philosophies, advocating for financial literacy and investing in assets.

books.title: The Psychology of Money
books.author: Morgan Housel
books.description: Timeless lessons on wealth, greed, and happiness, exploring the strange ways people think about money.

books.title: Sapiens
books.author: Yuval Noah Harari
books.description: A brief history of humankind that challenges everything we know about being human, from our origins to modern society.

books.title: The Diary of a Young Girl
books.author: Anne Frank
books.description: The wartime diary of Anne Frank, a Jewish girl hiding in Amsterdam during the Nazi occupation, a poignant testament to the human spirit.

books.title: Guns, Germs, and Steel
books.author: Jared Diamond
books.description: A study of why some civilizations came to dominate others, exploring the geographic and environmental factors that shaped history.

books.title: Homo Deus
books.author: Yuval Noah Harari
books.description: A look at the future of humanity once we surpass the limitations that have defined our species for millennia.

books.title: The Art of War
books.author: Sun Tzu
books.description: An ancient Chinese military treatise on strategy, tactics, and the philosophy of conflict, applicable to business and everyday life.

books.title: Book of Five Rings
books.author: Miyamoto Musashi
books.description: A classic text on Japanese swordsmanship and strategy by legendary samurai Miyamoto Musashi, applicable to any competitive endeavor.

books.title: A Brief History of Time
books.author: Stephen Hawking
books.description: A landmark in science writing exploring the cosmos, the Big Bang, black holes, and the fundamental laws governing the universe.

books.title: A Short History of Nearly Everything
books.author: Bill Bryson
books.description: An accessible and witty tour through the history of science, covering the Big Bang, evolution, and the rise of civilization.

books.title: Steve Jobs
books.author: Walter Isaacson
books.description: The exclusive biography of Apple co-founder Steve Jobs, drawn from over 40 interviews with Jobs himself.

books.title: Becoming
books.author: Michelle Obama
books.description: The memoir of the former First Lady of the United States, tracing her journey from Chicago's South Side to the White House.

books.title: Long Walk to Freedom
books.author: Nelson Mandela
books.description: The autobiography of Nelson Mandela, covering his childhood, his fight against apartheid, 27 years in prison, and his rise to presidency.

books.title: Elon Musk
books.author: Walter Isaacson
books.description: An intimate biography of Elon Musk exploring the polarizing entrepreneur behind Tesla, SpaceX, and X (formerly Twitter).

books.title: Leonardo da Vinci
books.author: Walter Isaacson
books.description: A biography of history's most creative genius, Leonardo da Vinci, drawing on 7,200 pages of his extraordinary notebooks.

books.title: The Design of Everyday Things
books.author: Don Norman
books.description: A powerful primer on how and why some products satisfy customers while others frustrate them, changing how you see designed objects.

books.title: Don't Make Me Think
books.author: Steve Krug
books.description: A classic guide to web usability explaining how real users interact with websites and how to design for intuitive, effortless navigation.

books.title: Logo Design Love
books.author: David Airey
books.description: A guide to creating iconic brand identities for designers, covering the process of designing lasting logos.

books.title: Strategic Writing for UX
books.author: Torrey Podmajersky
books.description: Drive engagement, conversion, and retention with every word. A guide to UX writing and content design strategy.

books.title: Web Design: Evolution
books.author: Multiple Authors
books.description: A comprehensive look at how web design has evolved from the early internet to today's modern practices.

books.title: Clean Code
books.author: Robert C. Martin
books.description: A handbook of agile software craftsmanship presenting best practices for writing clean, readable, and maintainable code.

books.title: The Pragmatic Programmer
books.author: Andrew Hunt
books.description: A guide covering the core processes of software development, helping programmers become more effective and pragmatic in their craft.

books.title: Zero to One
books.author: Peter Thiel
books.description: Notes on startups and how to build the future. Peter Thiel challenges conventional thinking about innovation and entrepreneurship.

books.title: Good to Great
books.author: Jim Collins
books.description: A research-driven study of how certain companies made the leap from good to truly great and sustained that greatness for 15+ years.

books.title: The Lean Startup
books.author: Eric Ries
books.description: A new approach to building companies and launching products that is changing how startups operate around the world.

books.title: Start with Why
books.author: Simon Sinek
books.description: Explores how leaders like Apple and Martin Luther King inspired action by starting with WHY rather than what or how.

books.title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
books.author: Daniel Kahneman
books.description: A groundbreaking exploration of the two systems that drive the way we think, make decisions, and understand the world.

books.title: One Year on a Bike
books.author: Martijn Doolaard
books.description: A stunning visual journey across Eurasia by bicycle from Amsterdam to Southeast Asia, with breathtaking photography.

books.title: Into the Wild
books.author: Jon Krakauer
books.description: The story of Chris McCandless who abandoned his possessions and hitchhiked to Alaska to live alone in the wilderness.

books.title: The Art of Travel
books.author: Alain de Botton
books.description: A philosophical meditation blending memoir and analysis on why we travel, what we seek, and what travel reveals about us.

books.title: 101 Amazing Switzerland
books.author: Various
books.description: Discover 101 incredible things to see and do in beautiful Switzerland, from alpine peaks to charming cities.

books.title: Crime and Punishment
books.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
books.description: A psychological novel following Raskolnikov, a poverty-stricken student who commits a murder and grapples with guilt and redemption.

books.title: The Count of Monte Cristo
books.author: Alexandre Dumas
books.description: The gripping tale of Edmond Dantès, wrongfully imprisoned, who escapes and executes an elaborate plan of revenge against those who betrayed him.

books.title: The Catcher in the Rye
books.author: J.D. Salinger
books.description: Teenager Holden Caulfield narrates his experiences after being expelled from prep school, exploring identity, alienation, and growing up.

books.title: One Hundred Years of Solitude
books.author: Gabriel Garcia Marquez
books.description: The multi-generational story of the Buendía family in the fictional town of Macondo, a landmark of magical realism.

books.title: Fahrenheit 451
books.author: Ray Bradbury
books.description: In a dystopian future where books are outlawed and burned, fireman Guy Montag begins to question the society he serves.

books.title: Neuromancer
books.author: William Gibson
books.description: The cyberpunk classic following a washed-up hacker hired for one last job deep into a world of artificial intelligence and crime.

books.title: I, Robot
books.author: Isaac Asimov
books.description: A collection of stories exploring the relationship between humans and robots, governed by Asimov's famous Three Laws of Robotics.

books.title: The Hunger Games
books.author: Suzanne Collins
books.description: In a dystopian future, teenager Katniss Everdeen volunteers for the televised Hunger Games fight-to-the-death competition to save her sister.

books.title: Divergent
books.author: Veronica Roth
books.description: In a future Chicago divided into factions, Beatrice Prior discovers she is Divergent and uncovers a conspiracy threatening her society.

books.title: Eragon
books.author: Christopher Paolini
books.description: A young farm boy named Eragon finds a mysterious stone that hatches into a dragon, beginning an epic journey of adventure and destiny.

books.title: Percy Jackson and the Lightning Thief
books.author: Rick Riordan
books.description: A 12-year-old boy discovers he is the son of a Greek god and embarks on a quest to prevent a war among the Olympians.

books.title: The Power of Habit
books.author: Charles Duhigg
books.description: Explains the science behind why habits exist and how they can be changed, drawing on research in neuroscience and psychology.

books.title: Outliers
books.author: Malcolm Gladwell
books.description: Examines the factors that contribute to high levels of success, exploring the stories of outliers and what makes them extraordinary.

books.title: The Tipping Point
books.author: Malcolm Gladwell
books.description: Explores how little things can make a big difference — the moment when ideas, trends, and social behaviours spread like wildfire.

books.title: Blink
books.author: Malcolm Gladwell
books.description: Investigates the power of thinking without thinking — how snap judgements and first impressions can be more powerful than careful deliberation.

books.title: Ikigai
books.author: Hector Garcia
books.description: The Japanese concept of ikigai — a reason for being — explored through the lives of the world's longest-living people in Okinawa.

books.title: The Selfish Gene
books.author: Richard Dawkins
books.description: A landmark work in evolutionary biology arguing that genes, not individuals or species, are the primary unit of natural selection.

books.title: Cosmos
books.author: Carl Sagan
books.description: A sweeping exploration of the universe and humanity's place in it, blending science, philosophy, and wonder in Carl Sagan's signature style.

books.title: Freakonomics
books.author: Steven Levitt
books.description: A rogue economist explores the hidden side of everything, applying economic thinking to topics ranging from drug dealing to parenting.

books.title: I Am Malala
books.author: Malala Yousafzai
books.description: The remarkable story of a young Pakistani girl who defied the Taliban to advocate for girls' education and survived an assassination attempt.

books.title: Open
books.author: Andre Agassi
books.description: The candid autobiography of tennis legend Andre Agassi, revealing his love-hate relationship with tennis and his journey to redemption.

books.title: Me Before You
books.author: Jojo Moyes
books.description: A heartwarming and emotional love story about a small-town girl and a recently paralyzed man who opens her world in unexpected ways.

books.title: The Notebook
books.author: Nicholas Sparks
books.description: A timeless love story about two young people from different backgrounds who fall deeply in love during one magical summer in the 1940s.

books.title: The Subtle Art of Not Giving a F*ck
books.author: Mark Manson
books.description: A counterintuitive approach to living a good life, arguing that improving our lives hinges on choosing what truly matters to us.

books.title: Man Search for Meaning
books.author: Viktor Frankl
books.description: A Holocaust survivor and psychiatrist describes his psychotherapeutic method of finding meaning in all forms of existence, even the most brutal ones.
